questions.q: After which general election was the Janata Party government formed?
questions.explanation: The Janata Party government was formed after the 1977 general election.

questions.q: Who became the Prime Minister of the Janata Party government in 1977?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai became Prime Minister at the head of the Janata Party government in 1977.

questions.q: The Janata Party government was notable as the first Union government not led by which party?
questions.explanation: It was the first Union government not led by the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: What did the Janata Party government's coming to power mark for the first time in independent India?
options: The first dissolution of the Lok Sabha
The first peaceful transfer of power to an opposition coalition
questions.explanation: It marked the first peaceful transfer of power to an opposition coalition at the Union level.

questions.q: Morarji Desai holds the distinction of being the first Prime Minister from outside which party?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai was the first Prime Minister not from the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: In which year did the Janata Party come to power at the Union level?
questions.explanation: The Janata Party came to power in 1977.

questions.q: The Janata Party government came to power immediately after which period ended?
questions.explanation: The 1977 election that brought the Janata Party to power followed the lifting of the 1975-77 Emergency.

questions.q: The Janata Party government was a government of what kind?
questions.explanation: The Janata Party government was an opposition coalition government.

questions.q: Which event is most commonly cited as the backdrop against which the Janata Party won the 1977 election?
options: The creation of a new state
questions.explanation: The Janata Party contested the 1977 election against the backdrop of the Emergency that had just been lifted.

questions.q: At which level of government was the Janata Party government formed in 1977?
options: At the level of a single state
questions.explanation: The Janata Party government was formed at the Union level.

questions.q: Morarji Desai's tenure as Prime Minister of the Janata Party government began in which year?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai became Prime Minister in 1977.

questions.q: Who succeeded Morarji Desai as Prime Minister in July 1979 after the Janata coalition fractured?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh became Prime Minister in July 1979 after Morarji Desai resigned.

questions.q: Which constitutional amendment, enacted in 1978 during the Janata-era government, reformed the emergency provisions?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment of 1978 was enacted during the Janata Party government to reform emergency provisions.

questions.q: Charan Singh served as Prime Minister from July 1979 until which month and year?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh served as Prime Minister from July 1979 to January 1980.

questions.q: The Janata Party government was formed by parties that had united in opposition to which period of governance?
questions.explanation: The constituent parties of the Janata Party had united in opposition to the Emergency.

questions.q: Who served as a Deputy Prime Minister in the Janata Party government formed in 1977?
questions.explanation: Jagjivan Ram served as a Deputy Prime Minister in the Janata Party government.

questions.q: The commission set up by the Janata Party government to examine excesses of the preceding Emergency was named after whom?
questions.explanation: The Shah Commission was appointed by the Janata Party government to inquire into the Emergency period.

questions.q: The Janata Party government's tenure at the Union level lasted for approximately how long?
questions.explanation: The Janata Party government lasted from 1977 until the 1980 election, under three years.

questions.q: Which general election in 1980 followed the fall of the Janata Party government?
questions.explanation: The 1980 general election was the seventh general election, held after the Janata government fell.

questions.q: Morarji Desai, the head of the Janata Party government, had earlier held which senior portfolio in a Congress government?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai had earlier served as Finance Minister in a Congress government.

questions.q: The Janata Party government's formation demonstrated which principle of parliamentary democracy?
options: Alternation of power between governing and opposition formations
questions.explanation: It demonstrated the peaceful alternation of power to an opposition formation through elections.

questions.q: Charan Singh, who later headed the government, is associated with championing the interests of which group?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh was known for his focus on rural and agrarian issues, championing farmers.

questions.q: Charan Singh became Prime Minister in 1979 but is noted for not having done what during his brief tenure?
options: Facing the Lok Sabha for a confidence vote
questions.explanation: Charan Singh resigned without facing the Lok Sabha for a vote of confidence.

questions.q: The 44th Amendment, passed during the Janata era, restored the normal duration of the Lok Sabha to how many years after it had been altered earlier?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment restored the normal term of the Lok Sabha to five years.

questions.q: Which earlier constituent of the Janata Party had its roots in the Bharatiya Jana Sangh?
options: Former Jana Sangh members within the Janata Party
questions.explanation: Former members of the Bharatiya Jana Sangh were among the constituents that merged into the Janata Party.

questions.q: The Janata Party government was supported in its formation partly by the movement led by which leader before 1977?
questions.explanation: Jayaprakash Narayan's movement of the mid-1970s helped consolidate the opposition that formed the Janata Party.

questions.q: The 44th Amendment, enacted under the Janata government, restored which right's protection that had earlier been altered?
options: The right to vote was extended
The right to education was added
The right to property as a fundamental right was removed from Part III
The right to information was created
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment removed the right to property from the list of fundamental rights, making it a legal right under Article 300A.

questions.q: After the Janata Party government fell, the 1980 Lok Sabha election resulted in a return to power of which formation?
questions.explanation: The 1980 general election resulted in a return of a Congress-led government at the Union level.

questions.q: Morarji Desai is also distinguished as the only Indian to have received which foreign honour?
options: The highest civilian honour of the United States
The highest civilian honour of the United Kingdom
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai is the only Indian to have received Pakistan's highest civilian honour, the Nishan-e-Pakistan.

questions.q: The Janata Party government's experience is often cited in Indian political history as an early example of what?
options: Coalition government at the Union level
questions.explanation: The Janata Party government is cited as an early example of coalition government at the Union level.
